page: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an entry-level virtual machine within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical AMD EPYC core with 2 threads per core, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and is supported by 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes a single complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a highly polarized capability profile. The instance achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 10% with a score of 3434.78. However, its multi-core stress-ng performance drops to average levels at 6819.14. Memory bandwidth is highly variable; small-block cached reads are weak at 326.91 GB/sec, while large-block uncached reads and writes perform at average levels of 70.08 GB/sec and 63.38 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show strong results, with gzip compression reaching 32.16 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.65 GB/sec. Conversely, database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are weak to poor, with web serving limited to 2.49 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest 4.0 GB memory capacity, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require bursty single-core compute capabilities without demanding sustained multi-core execution or high memory density. The bundled NVMe storage provides fast local access, but the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak database performance make it less suitable for heavy database hosting or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for lightweight background workers, single-threaded utility scripts, basic software compilation, or development environments.
description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an entry-level instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, though its multi-core performance and database operations remain in the average to weak categories. Memory bandwidth is moderate for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that prioritize single-threaded processing over sustained multi-core throughput. It is best suited for lightweight background tasks, basic software compilation, and development environments rather than high-traffic web serving or database hosting.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core compute performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared AMD EPYC instance featuring strong single-core performance and fast local NVMe storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
Best suited for lightweight background tasks and development environments
